data.title: Australia's Feb unemployment rate remains unchanged at 4.1%, aligning with expectations
data.description:
Australia’s unemployment rate held steady at 4.1% in February, but employment unexpectedly declined, raising concerns about labor market softness. 

The number of unemployed people dropped by 11.2 thousand to 612,600, with those seeking full-time jobs falling 11.3 thousand to 411.2 thousand while those looking for part-time jobs rose by 0.1 thousand to 201.4 thousand. 

Employment in Australia declined by 52.8 thousand in February 2025, missing market estimates of a 30 thousand gain and marking the first drop since March 2024. This followed a downwardly revised 30.5 thousand increase in the previous month.

Market expectations for the next RBA rate cut remain divided, with some analysts forecasting a move as early as May, while others anticipate it in July or August.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index gained 1.16% to close at 7,919 on Thursday, rebounding from the previous session’s losses. The Australian dollar fell below $0.635 on Thursday, marking its third straight decline, as traders reassessed the Reserve Bank of Australia’s monetary policy outlook following weaker-than-expected jobs data. 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWA), (NYSEARCA:FXA), (NYSEARCA:FLAU).

More on Australia

  • RBA cuts its cash rate by 25 bps for first time since 2020, in-line with market expectations
  • Australia's manufacturing downturn deepens in December
  • Australia's jobless rate falls to 8-month low of 3.9% in November
  • Reserve Bank of Australia holds cash rate steady at 4.35%, adopts mildly dovish stance
  • Australia's economy grows at weaker-than-expected pace in Q3; services activity remains in expansion

data.title: VEON GAAP EPADS of $5.73, revenue of $4B; initiates FY25 outlook, to initiate buyback
data.description:
• VEON press release (NASDAQ:VEON): FY GAAP EPADS of $5.73.
  • Revenue of $4B (+8.1% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA for the year was USD 1,691 million, representing a 4.9% year-on-year increase in the reported currency and a 12.0% increase in the underlying local currency.
  • Capex in 2024 increased 26.9% YoY, with a capex intensity of 20.6% (+3.0 p.p. YoY, 18.7% excl. Ukraine) for the year.
  • Total cash and cash equivalents and deposits as of 31 December 2024 amounted to USD 1,691 million.
  • Net debt to EBITDA (excluding lease liabilities) declined to 1.34x (from 1.52x as of 31st December 2023).
  • For FY25, VEON is guiding for underlying local currency growth for total revenue of between 12% and 14% year-on-year, and underlying EBITDA growth of between 13-15% year-on-year. VEON’s 2025 outlook for the Group’s capex intensity is in the range of 17%-19%.
  • VEON will also shortly commence the second phase of its previously announced share buyback program with respect to the Company’s American Depositary Shares. This second phase of the buyback will be in the amount of up to USD 35 million.

data.title: People’s Bank of China keeps lending rates unchanged, in line with expectations
data.description:
People’s Bank of China has held its one-year loan prime rate at 3.1% and the five-year rate at 3.6%, providing no fresh catalyst for markets to rally.

The key lending rates remain unchanged for the fifth consecutive month in March, in line with market expectations.

Both rates are at record lows following reductions in October and July last year.

The Shanghai Composite fell 0.2% to 3,420, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.3% to 10,940 on Thursday, extending losses from the previous session as the People’s Bank of China left key lending rates unchanged, in line with expectations, and the offshore yuan edged lower to around 7.23 per dollar as investors digested the latest monetary policy decision from the People’s Bank of China.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

More on China markets and economy

China’s retail sales accelerate 4.0% Y/Y; industrial output grow 5.9% y/y exceeds forecasts; unveils special action plan

China’s CPI fell for first time in 13 months, PPI deflation persists

China's trade surplus surpasses forecasts, exports grow by 2.3% and imports shrink

China hits back with additional tariffs up to 15% after Trump's latest move

China’s manufacturing PMI drops to 50.1 in January, missing expectations

data.title: Musk’s X raises almost $1 billion in new equity funding - report
data.description:
Elon Musk’s social media platform X (Formerly Twitter) has raised close to $1 billion in new equity from investors, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Musk, who is the CEO of EV giant Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) also participated in the equity raise, and the company is considering using some of the proceeds to pay down its remaining debt load, the report said.

The deal values X’s equity at roughly $32 billion.

Musk's Twitter buyout included at least $12.5 billion in debt, meaning the latest fundraising was completed at roughly the same $44 billion enterprise value as the tech billionaire's initial purchase, the report said.

Darsana Capital Partners, which bought some of X’s debt earlier this year, reportedly participated in the equity round. The investment firm 1789 Capital, which has backed xAI and SpaceX, also invested, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

Fidelity Investments, the asset manager that helped Musk acquire the platform, had reduced the valuation of its stake in X by 79% in September, compared to its worth at the time of Musk’s acquisition.

data.title: SoftBank buys chipmaker Ampere Computing for $6.5 billion in all-cash deal
Enerflex announces normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Enerflex (NYSE:EFXT) Wednesday said that its Board has given the nod to implement a Normal Course Issuer Bid.
  • The company intends to apply to the Toronto Stock Exchange to implement an NCIB that would permit the company to purchase for cancellation, common shares representing up to 5% of the public float over twelve months.

data.title: First Nordic Metals to commence trading on Nasdaq First North Growth Market in Sweden
data.description:
• First Nordic Metals (OTCQB:FNMCF) has received final approval for the listing of its Swedish depository receipts, a financial instrument issued by a Swedish bank representing shares in a non-Swedish company, on Nasdaq First North Growth Market in Sweden.
  • The company's SDRs will commence trading on Nasdaq First North at market open on March 21.

data.title: X Financial Non-GAAP EPADS of $1.16, revenue of $234.09M; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• X Financial press release (NYSE:XYF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of $1.16.
  • Revenue of $234.09M (+43.3% Y/Y).
  • The total loan amount facilitated and originated[1] in the fourth quarter of 2024 was RMB32,297 million, compared with RMB26,134 million in the same period of 2023.
  • The total loan amount facilitated and originated in 2024 was RMB104,889 million, compared with RMB105,557 million in 2023.
  • The total number of active borrowers[2] was 2,120,068 in the fourth quarter of 2024, compared with 1,603,760 in the same period of 2023.
  • The total number of active borrowers was 5,231,887 in 2024, compared with 4,495,997 in 2023.
  • The total outstanding loan balance[3] as of December 31, 2024 was RMB52,327 million, compared with RMB48,847 million as of December 31, 2023.
  • The delinquency rate for all outstanding loans that are past due for 31-60 days[4] as of December 31, 2024 was 1.17%, compared with 1.57% as of December 31, 2023.
  • The delinquency rate for all outstanding loans that are past due for 91-180 days[5] as of December 31, 2024 was 2.48%, compared with 3.12% as of December 31, 2023.

  • Business Outlook

    The company expects the total loan amount facilitated and originated for the first quarter of 2025 to be between RMB33.5 billion and RMB34.5 billion. The total loan amount facilitated and originated for 2025 is expected to be between RMB134.4 billion and RMB138.4 billion.
